site.name: LessonScriptor
site.tagline: Transcription IA en direct pour toute vidéo dans Chrome
site.description: Transcrivez n'importe quelle vidéo en temps réel dans Chrome. Mode gratuit illimité. Modifiez, surlignez et exportez vos notes. Idéal pour les étudiants, les cours et l'enseignement à distance.

hero.subtitle: LessonScriptor transcribes your lectures automatically — so you can focus on understanding, not writing. Built for students, ADHD learners, and anyone who learns better by reading along.
hero.waitlistNote: Free · No spam · Launch alert only

problem.subtitle: Lectures move fast. Notes fall behind. You end up choosing between understanding and capturing — and either way, something gets lost.

items.title: Hand cramps, missed words
items.desc: Writing fast enough to keep up means your notes are incomplete — or illegible.

items.title: ADHD makes it harder
items.desc: Splitting focus between listening and writing is exhausting. Something always slips.

items.title: Non-native language
items.desc: Following a fast lecture in your second language is double the cognitive load.

items.title: No notes = no studying
items.desc: Without written notes, reviewing means watching the whole lecture again.

origin.lede: Victoria is studying for a bachelor's in anthropology at a French university — entirely by distance, with ADHD. Hours of video lectures, workshops, seminars every week.
origin.p1: Taking notes while watching meant losing the thread. Rewinding, rewriting, rewatching. Notes too sparse to study from, or too rushed to read. The cognitive load of listening and writing at the same time, with ADHD, is brutal.
origin.p2: Pierre is a geek. So he started building. Victoria tested every version, gave feedback, broke things, explained what was missing. Weeks of iteration — a real back-and-forth.
origin.close: This extension is the result. We hope it lets you focus on what actually matters — understanding what you're learning, not fighting to write it down.
origin.sig: Victoria (testing & feedback) · Pierre (dev) · South of France · 2026

who.title: Built for people who learn by reading along

items.title: Students
items.desc: University lectures, online courses, MOOCs — capture everything without losing the thread of what's being said.

items.title: ADHD Learners
items.desc: Reading along while listening dramatically reduces cognitive load. Stop losing focus mid-lecture.

items.title: Online Course Takers
items.desc: Udemy, Coursera, YouTube tutorials — turn any video into searchable, editable notes automatically.

items.title: Accuracy over speed
items.desc: Audio is chunked into 10-second windows (3 seconds optimal) — exactly how the AI model was trained. The trade-off: a short delay, but far more accurate text than any real-time alternative.

items.title: Rich note editing
items.desc: Format your transcript as it arrives — highlight key phrases, bold important terms, add bullet points, and toggle headers on the fly.

items.title: Export anywhere
items.desc: Download as .txt or .md, or copy to clipboard. Your notes, your format.

items.title: 14 languages
items.desc: English, French, Spanish, Arabic, Hindi and more. Transcribe in the language of your lecture.

items.title: Privacy first
items.desc: Everything stays local by default — no account, no cloud processing in free mode. Your audio, your data.

items.title: Timestamps + history
items.desc: Optional timestamps on every line. Resume past sessions without losing your work.

worksEverywhere.subtitle: University internal platforms, protected videos, public sites — if audio comes out of your computer, we can transcribe it.

platforms.title: University platforms
platforms.desc: Moodle, Canvas, Blackboard, Panopto — the ones with no download, no subtitles, and no help. If the video plays in your browser, we transcribe it.

platforms.title: Public video sites
platforms.desc: YouTube, Coursera, Udemy — all the classics. But also any embedded player on any site, no matter how obscure or non-standard.

platforms.title: Works with headphones
platforms.desc: AI mode captures tab audio directly from the browser — no microphone, no speakers needed. Study at 2am without disturbing anyone.

worksEverywhere.explainerEyebrow: How the two modes capture audio
worksEverywhere.explainerFree: Free mode uses your microphone to pick up audio through your speakers. Works on every platform — but volume matters. Turn system volume up for best accuracy.
worksEverywhere.explainerAI: AI mode captures the browser tab audio directly, bypassing your mic entirely. Clean capture with headphones on, at any volume, on any protected platform.

pricing.subtitle: Start for free with your microphone. Top up when you need AI-powered transcription — no subscription, no expiry.
pricing.whyPayDesc: Free mode uses your browser's built-in speech recognition — local, instant, free. Premium sends your audio to a state-of-the-art cloud AI model. That processing has a real cost. We pass it on transparently: credits are consumed second-by-second from your pack, they never expire, and you choose how fast you burn them based on the transcription mode you use.
pricing.freeDesc: Uses your browser's built-in speech engine and your microphone. Fully local — no audio sent anywhere.
pricing.freeCaveat: Audio must play through your speakers — the mic needs to hear it.
pricing.noSubCallout: No subscription. No "I forgot to cancel" tax. Credits never expire.
pricing.costCallout: Buy a pack once. Your hours are consumed second-by-second as you transcribe — nothing is wasted when you stop. Time shown is for Normal mode. Live mode sends audio more frequently and burns ~3× faster. The extension shows your live burn rate so there are no surprises.
pricing.headphonesCallout: Works with headphones on. Tab audio is captured directly — no microphone, no speakers needed.
premiumFeatures: Cloud AI engine — far better accuracy than browser speech
Direct tab audio — works with any headphones
Higher accuracy across accents & background noise
Premium formatting — highlights, headers, hide/show sections
Player sync — transcript follows the video position (beta)
Hours consumed second-by-second — nothing wasted when you stop
Credits never expire — top up whenever you want
10-min grace period if credits run out mid-lecture
pricing.footnote: Buy once, use whenever. A full semester at 3h/week on normal mode costs around €18 — less than one textbook chapter.

quickstart.subtitle: No account. No configuration. Just install and go.

steps.title: Install the extension
steps.desc: Add it to Chrome from the Web Store. No sign-up required.

steps.title: Open any video
steps.desc: Go to YouTube, Coursera, Udemy — any site with a video lecture.

steps.title: Hit Record
steps.desc: Click the extension icon, open the side panel, press Record. That's it.

items.a: Buy a credit pack and you'll receive a license key by email. Open the extension settings, enter the key, and your credits activate immediately. They're consumed second-by-second as you transcribe — nothing is wasted when you stop.

items.q: I lost my activation code. What do I do?
items.a: Check your inbox — the key was sent when you purchased. Your key is portable: it works on any computer, survives reinstalls, and your remaining credits are always preserved. Think of it as a prepaid balance, not a device licence.

items.q: My transcription is in the wrong language.
items.a: Make sure you've selected the correct language in the extension settings before starting. If you changed language mid-session, stop the transcription, select the right language, and start again. The speech engine needs a clean restart to switch languages.

items.q: The transcription isn't working at all.
items.a: Try these steps in order: (1) Make sure the extension is up to date — check for updates in chrome://extensions. (2) Reload the extension by toggling it off and on. (3) Reload the page with the video. (4) Check your internet connection — AI mode requires it. (5) If you're using mic mode, make sure your system volume is high enough (80%+) and that you've granted the extension permission to access your microphone.

items.q: Microphone mode isn't picking up the audio.
items.a: This is almost always a volume issue. Free mode relies on your mic hearing your speakers — the louder your system volume, the better the pickup. MacBook Air users typically need a higher volume than MacBook Pro users. Also make sure you've allowed microphone access for the extension.

items.q: Can I transcribe multiple videos at the same time?
items.a: Yes — but only with AI mode. AI mode captures tab audio per tab, so each video runs independently. Free (mic) mode captures whatever your speakers play, so mixing audio sources will confuse the transcription.

Player sync is a beta feature for Premium users. It links the transcript to the video timeline — when you click a line in the transcript, the video jumps to that moment. When the video plays, the transcript follows along. It works best with standard HTML5 video players.

items.q: What are the premium formatting features?
items.a: Premium unlocks additional formatting tools: text highlighting with colours, toggling section headers on and off, and hiding/collapsing parts of your transcript. These work on top of the free editing features (bold, bullets, auto-save) and are designed for students who want to turn a raw transcript into study-ready notes.

contact.subtitle: This extension was built out of a real need. If you have ideas, run into a bug, or just want to say hi — we read everything.

options.title: Feature suggestions
options.desc: Something missing? Tell us what would make this better for you.

options.title: Bug reports
options.desc: Something broken? We want to know — the more detail the better.

options.title: Just saying hi
options.desc: We genuinely love hearing how people use it. Drop us a note.

youtube.h1: Transcrire une vidéo YouTube en temps réel
youtube.subtitle: Obtenez une transcription en direct et modifiable de n'importe quelle vidéo YouTube directement dans Chrome — sans copier-coller ni changer d'onglet. Mode gratuit inclus.
youtube.answer: Pour transcrire une vidéo YouTube dans Chrome : installez l'extension Lessonscriptor, ouvrez votre vidéo YouTube, cliquez sur l'icône du panneau latéral et appuyez sur lecture. La transcription démarre automatiquement et apparaît en temps réel à côté de la vidéo.

methods.title: How to get a YouTube video transcript — all methods compared

items.cons: Read-only, no editing, not available on all videos, inaccurate on auto-generated captions

items.pros: Live editing, highlight, export, works on any video not just YouTube, 14+ languages, free mode

items.cons: No live transcription, not real-time, no editing, YouTube-only

steps.title: Step-by-step: transcribe a YouTube video with Lessonscriptor
items: Install Lessonscriptor from the Chrome Web Store (free, one click, no sign-up).
Open any YouTube video in Chrome.
Click the Lessonscriptor icon in your Chrome toolbar to open the side panel.
Hit play on the video — transcription starts automatically.
Edit as you watch: click to add notes, highlight important passages, delete tangents.
Export to Markdown when done — your formatted notes are ready.

items.q: Puis-je télécharger la transcription d'une vidéo YouTube ?
items.a: Oui. Avec Lessonscriptor, vous pouvez exporter la transcription en Markdown (.md) ou la copier dans le presse-papiers pour la coller dans Notion, Google Docs ou Obsidian. L'export préserve tout votre formatage.

items.q: Fonctionne-t-il sur les vidéos YouTube sans sous-titres ?
items.a: Oui. Lessonscriptor ne dépend pas des sous-titres de YouTube — il transcrit l'audio réel en temps réel, que YouTube ait ou non généré des sous-titres automatiques.

items.q: Peut-on transcrire une vidéo YouTube en français ?
items.a: Oui. Sélectionnez le français dans les paramètres de l'extension et la transcription apparaîtra en français.

liveCaptions.h1: Sous-titres en direct pour toute vidéo dans Chrome
liveCaptions.subtitle: Ajoutez des sous-titres IA en temps réel et modifiables à toute vidéo dans Chrome. Gratuit, privé, compatible avec toutes les plateformes.
liveCaptions.answer: Lessonscriptor ajoute des sous-titres IA en direct à toute vidéo dans Chrome via un panneau latéral. Contrairement aux sous-titres en direct intégrés à Chrome, ceux-ci sont entièrement modifiables, exportables et prennent en charge 14+ langues.

vsChrome.title: Lessonscriptor vs Chrome's built-in Live Captions
rows: Yes — click to jump to moment

items.q: Comment ajouter des sous-titres en direct dans Chrome ?
items.a: Installez Lessonscriptor depuis le Chrome Web Store. Ouvrez n'importe quelle vidéo, cliquez sur l'icône de l'extension pour ouvrir le panneau latéral, et appuyez sur lecture. Les sous-titres en direct apparaissent automatiquement.

items.q: Chrome dispose-t-il de sous-titres en direct intégrés ?
items.a: Oui, Chrome propose une fonctionnalité de sous-titres de base (Paramètres → Accessibilité → Sous-titres en direct). Cependant, elle ne supporte que l'anglais, produit un flux en lecture seule non modifiable et ne gère pas bien le vocabulaire technique. Lessonscriptor supporte 14+ langues et offre une transcription entièrement modifiable et exportable.

transcribeVideo.h1: Transcrire une vidéo en texte — gratuitement, dans votre navigateur
transcribeVideo.subtitle: Convertissez n'importe quelle vidéo en texte en temps réel avec l'IA. Sans upload, sans attente, sans abonnement. Fonctionne directement dans Chrome.
transcribeVideo.answer: Pour transcrire gratuitement une vidéo en texte dans Chrome : installez Lessonscriptor, ouvrez n'importe quelle vidéo et appuyez sur lecture. L'IA transcrit l'audio en temps réel dans un panneau latéral. Le mode gratuit est illimité.

items.q: Comment transcrire gratuitement une vidéo en texte ?
items.a: Installez Lessonscriptor (extension Chrome gratuite). Ouvrez n'importe quelle vidéo dans Chrome. Cliquez sur l'icône de l'extension pour ouvrir le panneau latéral. Appuyez sur lecture — la transcription démarre automatiquement. Le mode gratuit est gratuit pour toujours.

items.q: Puis-je convertir un fichier MP4 en texte ?
items.a: Oui. Ouvrez le fichier MP4 dans Chrome (glissez-déposez dans un onglet Chrome) puis utilisez Lessonscriptor pour le transcrire en temps réel.

otterAlt.h1: La meilleure alternative à Otter.ai pour les étudiants
otterAlt.subtitle: Vous cherchez une alternative à Otter.ai qui fonctionne dans votre navigateur, sans abonnement, et gratuite au quotidien ? C'est Lessonscriptor.
otterAlt.answer: Lessonscriptor est la meilleure alternative à Otter.ai pour les étudiants car il fonctionne dans Chrome sur toute plateforme vidéo (pas seulement les réunions), dispose d'un mode gratuit illimité, et facture environ 1 €/heure pour le premium — sans abonnement mensuel.

items.q: Quelle est la meilleure alternative gratuite à Otter.ai ?
items.a: Lessonscriptor est la meilleure alternative gratuite à Otter.ai pour les étudiants. Son mode gratuit est illimité et sans restriction mensuelle.

items.q: Existe-t-il une alternative à Otter.ai qui fonctionne sur YouTube ?
items.a: Oui — Lessonscriptor. Contrairement à Otter.ai, conçu pour les réunions, Lessonscriptor fonctionne sur toute vidéo dans Chrome, y compris YouTube, Coursera, Udemy et les portails universitaires.

lecture.h1: Comment transcrire automatiquement des vidéos de cours
lecture.intro: La façon la plus rapide de transcrire automatiquement une vidéo de cours est d'utiliser Lessonscriptor — une extension Chrome gratuite qui transcrit n'importe quelle vidéo en temps réel, directement dans votre navigateur. Sans upload, sans attente, sans abonnement.

sections.h2: Why manual note-taking during lectures fails
sections.body: Writing by hand during a lecture forces you to split your attention between listening, understanding, and writing — often all at the same time. Research consistently shows that students who transcribe lectures word-for-word retain less of the content than those who actively engage with it. The problem isn't discipline. It's the tool. When you're busy writing, you're not learning.

sections.h2: How AI transcription works in a browser
sections.body: Lessonscriptor uses two transcription engines. The free mode uses the Web Speech API, which is built into Chrome and processes audio locally on your device — nothing ever leaves your computer. The premium mode uses Grok (xAI), which captures the audio directly from the browser tab and sends it to the API for transcription, returning highly accurate text within seconds.

sections.h2: Step-by-step: how to transcribe a lecture video with Lessonscriptor
steps: Install Lessonscriptor from the Chrome Web Store — it's free, no sign-up required.
Open your lecture video in Chrome (YouTube, Coursera, Zoom recording, university portal, or any video platform).
Click the Lessonscriptor icon in your Chrome toolbar to open the side panel.
Hit play on the video — transcription starts automatically and text appears in real-time.
Edit as you watch: click to add your own notes, highlight key concepts in color, bold terminology, delete tangents.
Click any timestamp in the transcript to jump back to that exact moment in the video.
When done, export to Markdown to get a formatted document with all your highlights and formatting.

sections.h2: Free mode vs Premium mode — which to use for lectures
sections.body: For most students, free mode is enough. It works well in a quiet environment with speakers on. If you prefer headphones, or if you're transcribing content with heavy technical vocabulary, accents, or fast speech, Premium mode gives significantly better accuracy at about €1 per hour.

sections.h2: How to study from your transcript
sections.body: Once transcribed, treat your Lessonscriptor document like a study guide. Highlight the most important concepts in yellow. Bold key terminology. Add your own interpretation in brackets as you review. Export to Markdown and import into Notion or Obsidian for long-term storage. Search the full text before exams.

items.q: Puis-je transcrire automatiquement un enregistrement Zoom ?
items.a: Oui. Ouvrez l'enregistrement Zoom dans Chrome et utilisez Lessonscriptor pour le transcrire. Il fonctionne sur toute vidéo lue dans Chrome.

items.q: Quel est le meilleur outil de transcription pour les étudiants ?
items.a: Lessonscriptor est le meilleur outil de transcription pour les étudiants car il fonctionne directement dans Chrome sur toute plateforme vidéo, offre un mode gratuit illimité et permet de modifier la transcription en direct pendant le visionnage.
